title: error creating libpod runtime: error configuring CNI network plugin: failed to add watch on "/etc/cni/net.d/": no space left on device
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->
**kind bug**

**Description**
After playing around with podman a bit yesterday, trying to build a few images and running buildah demos, I can now no longer run commands such as `podman ps`. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
I am shaky as to what caused this but my podman is currently in this state, so it is constantly happening. What led up to this:
1. I ran this buildah example: https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/demos/buildah-scratch-demo.sh  but I believe I stopped the container
2. Played around a bit with building some images, Had no issues until the next day.
3. Now running `podman ps` causes the issue.

**Describe the results you received:**

error creating libpod runtime: error configuring CNI network plugin: failed to add watch on "/etc/cni/net.d/": no space left on device


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
getenforce: enforcing
**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.9.1
Go Version:    go1.10.4
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
could not get runtime: error configuring CNI network plugin: failed to add watch on "/etc/cni/net.d/": no space left on device
;)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora 28

comments.body:
I assume you're not out of hard drive space per `df -h`?

If not, it's probably a result of running out of inotify instances. Does `ps aux | grep podman` show many processes?

No, not out of disk space: 
```
Filesystem                               Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
devtmpfs                                 7.7G     0  7.7G   0% /dev
tmpfs                                    7.7G   86M  7.6G   2% /dev/shm
tmpfs                                    7.7G  2.3M  7.7G   1% /run
tmpfs                                    7.7G     0  7.7G   0% /sys/fs/cgroup
/dev/mapper/fedora_localhost--live-root   49G   19G   28G  40% /
tmpfs                                    7.7G   22M  7.7G   1% /tmp
/dev/nvme0n1p1                           976M  223M  687M  25% /boot
/dev/mapper/fedora_localhost--live-home  176G   27G  141G  16% /home
tmpfs                                    1.6G   20K  1.6G   1% /run/user/42
tmpfs                                    1.6G   12M  1.6G   1% /run/user/1000
```

I forgot to mention that I checked: 
`sysctl user.max_inotify_instances`
result = 128
`fs.inotify.max_user_watches`
result=8192

I see no processes when I grep ps aux | grep podman.
/dev/mapper/fedora_localhost--live-home -- is this a special flavor of f28?

I think we need to build a way of disabling inotify into ocicni, and turn that on for Podman by default. Not as though we need to update the plugins available when we only ever launch a single container per process.

> is this a special flavor of f28?

No. It's a standard build. 

My issue resolved after reboot. I wish I could provide more info. If I manage to get podman into the same state again I will let you know.
I'm going to leave this open as a tracker for removing inotify by default - we don't need it, and it seems to occasionally cause issues like this.
fwiw, I think hit this when running `podman` as a non-privileged user; using `sudo podman` work just fine. Also, bumping up `user.max_inotify_instances` and `fs.inotify.max_user_watches` helps too.
@giuseppe I did not think we used CNI when running in non root mode?

We shouldn't. Looks like initialization isn't checking if we're rootless.

I'll add the check and see if that breaks anything.
